shots.title: TranscriptTurn types + JSONL utilities
shots.notes: TranscriptTurn/ToolCallSummary/TranscriptLine types. JSONL utilities: appendTurn, readTranscript, listTranscripts, getTranscriptPath. All sync fs, turn_number derived on read. 12 tests, all passed first run.

shots.title: PostToolUse transcript guard
shots.notes: PostToolUse guard with no matcher (fires on all tools). Per-tool param summarization, error detection from tool_response. Silent return, sync fs only. 13 tests passed first run.

shots.title: slope transcript CLI commands
shots.notes: list, show, stats subcommands. Per-tool failure breakdown and token data observability notice added after AI/ML review. 10 tests passed first run.

shots.title: Config, guard installation, exports, version bump
shots.notes: transcriptsPath config field, MCP registry entries for readTranscript/listTranscripts, version bump to 1.11.0. Full build + test + typecheck clean.

nutrition.description: Full build + test verified after each ticket and after review fixes. 1473 tests passing at completion, 0 failing.
7 commits: 4 feature, 1 codebase map, 1 architect review fix, 1 AI/ML review enhancement. All pushed before merge.
36 new tests across 3 test files (transcript core: 13, transcript guard: 13, transcript CLI: 10). Includes malformed JSONL resilience test.
Two review rounds (architect + AI/ML) caught 5 improvements: malformed line resilience, sort stability, concurrency docs, per-tool failure breakdown, token data notice. All addressed in same session.

nineteenth_hole.how_did_it_feel: Cleanest sprint in recent history. All 4 tickets landed in_the_hole on first attempt. The JSONL design made everything straightforward — append-only semantics eliminated concurrency complexity. Two review rounds added real value without finding structural issues.
nineteenth_hole.advice_for_next_player: Transcript guard fires on ALL tools (no matcher). If you add a new guard that also fires on all PostToolUse events, they'll share a hook entry in settings.json. The guard uses sync fs (appendFileSync) — don't add async operations without rethinking the guard path.
nineteenth_hole.what_surprised_you: The AI/ML review surfaced genuinely useful insights — per-tool failure breakdown and token data observability notice were quick wins that meaningfully improved the stats command. Two-perspective reviews (architect + ML) caught complementary issues.
nineteenth_hole.excited_about_next: Token counts when HookInput exposes them. Retry chain linking for failure recovery analysis. The JSONL foundation makes all of these additive — no schema migrations needed.

bunker_locations: PostToolUse guard with no matcher fires on EVERY tool call — be aware of this when adding similar guards
appendFileSync is single-agent safe but multi-agent sessions could interleave writes — documented in guard
yardage_book_updates: src/core/transcript.ts: JSONL utilities — appendTurn, readTranscript, listTranscripts, getTranscriptPath
src/cli/guards/transcript.ts: PostToolUse transcript guard (no matcher, fires on all tools)
src/cli/commands/transcript.ts: slope transcript list|show|stats CLI commands
course_management_notes: 4 tickets, par 4, score 4 — par. All shots in_the_hole, 0 hazards, 0 penalties.
Slope 2 confirmed appropriate — new infrastructure (JSONL files, new guard pattern) but familiar patterns.
6 new source files, 3 new test files, 7 modified files. Version bumped to 1.11.0.
Two review rounds (architect + AI/ML engineer) addressed 5 findings, all landed cleanly.
